mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, driving negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and rising real sales indicates demand-pull pressure that forces monetary tightening, generating negative forward equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with a t-statistic below 1.5, or returns are positive in >55% of co-occurring high-inflation high-sales periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index reflects weakening manufacturing output that reduces corporate earnings growth, producing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to positive during late-cycle expansions, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation (p > 0.10) with industrial production changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in real sales exceeding two percent indicates a sharp contraction in consumer demand that immediately pressures equity valuations downward over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold condition yields a negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of positive payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 280 signals entrenched inflation that mandates aggressive central bank rate hikes, compressing equity multiples and driving negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions (K-S test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and declining GDP growth indicates sector-specific overheating that precedes broader economic slowdown, producing negative forward equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of divergence periods.
